goal: Decide which items to hire professionals for vs DIY
Allocate the $300 budget between labor, vehicle, and protective materials
constraints: Move date fixed: Saturday in mid-June; move-in must occur in morning
Fourth-floor walk-up; no elevator; plan for stairs-only move
Limited manpower: only two friends available for light lifting; heavy items to be moved by professionals
Budget cap: about $300 for any hired help and rental equipment.
decisions: Hybrid approach: hire professionals for heavy/bulky or specialty items and DIY for the rest
Apply donation/sell/discard disposition rubric to decide whether to donate, sell, or trash items before moving
Obtain 2–3 quotes from movers; confirm stair fees, number of movers for a 4th-floor walk-up, and insurance/valuation options
Select budget allocation between labor, vehicle, and supplies by evaluating Scenario A or Scenario C; proceed with quotes using per-item pricing and confirm stair-fee structure
Prioritize professional movers for antique armoire; assess whether glass dining table top is removable to enable DIY
Obtain per-item quotes for armoire and glass table (stair carries) to compare against bundle/overall quotes
entities: stair carries equipment list (moving straps, stair-climbing dolly, heavy-duty furniture dolly, moving blankets, corner protectors, ram board, edge guards, stretch wrap, tape, gloves, basic tool kit)
banister and door frame protection protocol (wrap banisters, use edge guards, avoid adhesive damage)
loading plan for stairs and landings (sequence, spotters, rest breaks)
heavy/awkward items designated for professional move
largest items to move identified (2–4 items)
open: Order packing supplies (boxes, tape, bubble wrap, markers, stretch wrap)
Disassemble furniture that’s practical to take apart; bag and label hardware
Plan and book a cleaner or cleaning schedule
Take photos of emptied old place for deposit
Final walkthrough of old place and meter readings
Review and incorporate lease/landlord details into move plan
Assess stair-climbing equipment and professional help needs for walk-up
Measure stair width, landing dimensions, and door/frame widths; compare with furniture dimensions
Confirm availability of two light-lifting helpers on move day
Identify heavy items to hire pros for and list them for movers
Assess whether the glass dining table top is removable and safe to DIY with two people
Decide on pro vs DIY for antique armoire based on removable doors/shelves
Obtain per-item quotes for armoire and glass table (stair carries) to compare with general quotes
